channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: AI-Powered Discovery of Rosmarinic Acid as a Novel Ferroptosis Inhibitor for Ulcerative Colitis via Targeting the ALOX15-VDAC1 Axis.
item.description: Research (Washington, D.C.) - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with limited mechanism-based therapies, and ferroptosis-an iron-dependent lipid peroxidation-driven cell death-has emerged as a criti...

item.title: Enterococcus hirae Y-HS Alleviates Ulcerative Colitis by Activating PXR/Nrf2-mediated Metabolic-immune Crosstalk.
item.description: Probiotics and antimicrobial proteins - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with limited non-invasive biomarkers and variable responses to probiotics. This study investigates the probiotic potential of Enteroc...

item.title: Scleral image analysis via artificial intelligence to evaluate inflammatory status in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Among the extraintestinal ocular manifestations of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), episcleritis is associated with the inflammatory activity of IBD. The use of artificial intelligen...

item.title: TREM1 signaling amplifies neutrophil-mediated inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Nature communications - In inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), complex immune dysregulation induces chronic inflammation. TREM1 is a receptor expressed on myeloid cells and is activated by ligands such as PGLYRP1 to amplify...

item.title: Serum REG3α as a biomarker for clinical and endoscopic activity in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Scientific reports - Current biomarkers, including serum C-reactive protein (CRP) and fecal calprotectin, have limitations in monitoring disease activity of ulcerative colitis (UC). Regenerating islet-derived protein 3...

item.title: Association between hemoglobin-to-red cell distribution width ratio and occurrence of sepsis during ICU stay in inflammatory bowel disease patients who died: a retrospective study using the EICU-CRD database.
item.description: PloS one - The hemoglobin-to-red cell distribution width ratio (HRR) is a biomarker associated with systemic inflammation and outcomes in critical illness. Within clinical databases, there exists an extreme-p...

item.title: SHIP1 is required for T cell surveillance of occult malignancies.
item.description: Cellular and molecular life sciences : CMLS - SHIP1-deficient mice develop severe, transmural Crohn's-like ileitis and die prematurely. Recent analysis of a human Crohn's disease (CD) cohort revealed a significant subset of patients have sever...

item.title: Inflammasomes in Intestinal Inflammation: Guardians of Mucosal Homeostasis, Amplifiers of Disease, and Targets for Intervention.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - The intestinal mucosa is continuously exposed to dietary antigens, commensal microorganisms, and enteric pathogens, thereby depending on innate immune pathways that detect danger without compromisi...

item.title: Microbial metabolite Urolithin A protects against inorganic arsenic-induced gut barrier dysfunction in humanized AS3MT mice.
item.description: Gut microbes - Chronic exposure to inorganic arsenic (iAs) remains a major environmental health concern and is associated with significant gastrointestinal (GI) disorders, including gastroenteritis, diarrhea, and...

item.title: Optimizing bowel preparation regimens for colon capsule endoscopy: an umbrella review (overview of systematic reviews).
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastrointestinal endoscopy - Colon capsule endoscopy (CCE) is a minimally invasive alternative to colonoscopy, but its diagnostic performance depends entirely on adequate mucosal cleansing (adequate cleansing rate, ACR) and co...

item.title: TTC7A deficiency: A retrospective international study on treatment and outcomes from the Inborn Errors Working Party of EBMT.
item.description: Journal of human immunity - Tetratricopeptide repeat domain 7A (TTC7A) deficiency is a primary immunodeficiency due to mutations in the TTC7A gene. It causes intestinal disease and a poorly characterized immunodeficiency, wit...

item.title: Disparities in robot utilization in colorectal surgery: the widening gap.
item.description: Surgical endoscopy - Robot-assisted surgery has expanded in adoption and indication among multiple surgical specialties, including colorectal surgery. It is unclear if access to robotic surgery is increasing equally am...

item.title: Knowledge gaps in tubular gut tumours: a critical appraisal of the 6th edition of the World Health Organization classification of tumours.
item.description: The Journal of pathology - Review - Gastrointestinal cancer is a global health problem. In the new 6th edition of the World Health Organization Classification of Tumours (WCT) of the Digestive System, updated evidence and guidance is...

item.title: Short, not skinny: The distinct sarcopenia phenotype of pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis-inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Sarcopenia is prevalent in pediatric liver disease and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and predicts adverse outcomes. We aimed to compare sarcopenia prevalence in pediatric primary sclerosing chol...

item.title: Primary sclerosing cholangitis displays distinct colonic mucosa topography yet a shared mast cell state with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Nature communications - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic, progressing cholestatic disease that often co-occurs with inflammatory bowel disease (PSC-IBD). PSC-IBD affecting the colon (PSC-ulcerative coliti...

item.title: Recent Advances in the Pharmacological Management of Pediatric Spondyloarthritis.
item.description: Paediatric drugs - Review - Juvenile spondyloarthritis (JSpA) refers to a heterogeneous group of inflammatory diseases affecting peripheral joints and the axial skeleton during childhood and adolescence. Enthesitis-related ar...

item.title: Integration of genetic evidence to identify approved drug targets.
item.description: Genome medicine - Drugs targeting genes supported by human genetic evidence are more likely to succeed in clinical trials. While previous approaches have benchmarked individual methods such as genome-wide associatio...

item.title: A genetic variant of adenylate cyclase 7 associated with ulcerative colitis shows impaired function and G-protein-coupled receptor signaling.
item.description: Human genetics - A missense variant of adenylate cyclase 7 (AC7), p.Asp439Glu, has been significantly associated with ulcerative colitis (UC) in genome-wide association studies. Previous work suggested that this va...

item.title: FCGR2A promoter variant reveals shared genetic susceptibility between IBD and stroke.
item.description: Molecular and cellular biochemistry - While the epidemiological association between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and stroke is well-established, the shared genetic architecture underlying these diseases remains unclear. This study ...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease in the aging population.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - As the prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) rises rapidly among older adults, gastroenterologists and other health care providers increasingly face challenges in managing age-related como...
